career_highlights:
I work at the intersection of technology, business, and law to help mission-driven teams move from invention to adoption.

Highlights
	•	Built and exited a professional services business: founded a U.S. patent law firm, scaled it to a team of 15 lawyers, and sold it to a national firm.
	•	Enabled commercialization for deep-tech teams: advised founders and engineering-led companies on protecting and deploying breakthrough technology, with particular focus on defensible differentiation and market entry.
	•	Partnered with global innovators: worked with organizations of all sizes, including long-running relationships with major technology clients, translating complex engineering into strategy-aligned execution.
	•	Co-founded climate-tech ventures: helped originate and build multiple ventures aimed at meaningful emissions reduction and systems change. Click on the [Venture Name] link above for more info.
	•	Operator mindset, not just advisory: experience across venture formation, product and IP strategy, fundraising readiness, and go-to-market constraints that show up in the real world.
	•	Impact through ecosystem building: active in New Zealand’s innovation community through mentorship and founder support, with a focus on practical pathways from research to scalable outcomes.
how_im_looking_to_support_the_nz_ecosyst:
I’m focused on practical, high-leverage support that helps founders and teams move faster from capability to outcomes.
	•	Mentorship for technical founders: hands-on guidance on commercialization fundamentals, defensible differentiation, and turning engineering progress into a clear product and customer narrative.
	•	US and global market bridges: help teams position for the US market, including regulatory and go-to-market realities, customer discovery approaches, and investor expectations. I can also make targeted introductions where there is a strong fit.
	•	IP and strategy support that serves the business: pressure-test IP strategy against product direction, competitive landscape, and fundraising needs, so IP spend maps to real leverage.
	•	Venture formation and scaling support: advise on early operating cadence, milestone planning, hiring priorities, and board-level thinking, with an operator’s bias for execution.
	•	Support for research-to-venture pathways: work with universities, labs, and incubators to help translate research into licensing and spinout outcomes, including shaping the commercial story and early market validation.
	•	Time and talent: open to advisory or governance roles, guest sessions, and structured office hours. Where there’s strong alignment, I’m also open to collaborating directly with a venture in a hands-on capacity.
key_networks:
•	Sir Edmund Hillary Fellowship (EHF): founder community across Aotearoa New Zealand with a shared focus on impact-led ventures.
	•	University of Auckland innovation ecosystem: active in founder support and mentorship through the University of Auckland’s entrepreneurship community.
	•	U.S. and global technology networks: long-standing relationships across the U.S. innovation landscape, including founders, operators, and investors, with perspective on U.S. market dynamics and regulatory realities.
	•	Deep-tech commercialization community: connections across inventors, engineers, and commercialization professionals working to move research into licensing and spinout outcomes.
	•	Climate-tech and impact venture networks: relationships with builders and advisors working on emissions reduction and systems-change ventures across multiple geographies.
impact_goals_nz: My focus is on accelerating solutions that measurably improve climate and community resilience by helping teams do three things well: build technology that works, structure ventures that can survive, and get adoption fast enough to matter. I am especially interested in helping New Zealand and global founders bridge into larger markets, and in supporting ventures that can demonstrate real-world outcomes rather than just promising narratives.
how_to_support_my_work:
How to support my work
	•	Make introductions: I’m always looking to meet founders, researchers, operators, and investors working on climate-tech and other mission-led ventures with clear pathways to measurable outcomes.
	•	Share real problems: If you’re close to an industry pain point that needs a deep-tech solution, I’m interested in hearing it and helping connect the right people to explore it.
	•	Pilot and validation opportunities: If you can provide access to customers, data, test environments, or pilot sites, that support is often more valuable than funding.
	•	Mentorship and collaboration: I welcome collaborators who can contribute domain expertise, product leadership, engineering capability, or commercialization experience.
five_things_to_know:
I am a co-founder of REVOLUTION Turbine Technologies (RTT), which creates micro expansion turbines to generate clean electricity from wasted gas pressure.

I am also a co-founder of VORTE Technologies Ltd, which uses compressed air to replace traditional cooling equipment.

I have decades of experience helping companies of all sizes, including Microsoft and Amazon.com, with intellectual property issues.

name: Ling Wong
bio_tagline: Investor and Entrepreneur in Health and Technology
currently_based_in: Seattle, Washington  United States of America
career_highlights:
Dr. Ling Wong the Founder and General Partner of Highbury. Highbury was founded on the principle to be a true partner and champion to entrepreneurs, using science and technology to build companies that fundamentally transform the world for the betterment of humanity today, and in the future.

Dr. Wong draws from her unique background and experience as a scientist, entrepreneur, and investor to support and bring to market companies that are uniquely positioned to drive transformative change, while generating financial success.

Dr. Wong has always been passionate about seeking entrepreneurs and partners that are looking to disrupt the status quo in science and technology for the betterment of society.

On the investing front, Dr. Wong has been involved in some of the market’s most notable investments and exits including several $1B+ companies: Devoted Health, Orca Bio, Collective Health, Color Genomics, Apeel Sciences, Stripe, Offer Up, Rappi, and Branch Metrics. Highlight deals include: Anacor Pharmaceuticals (acquired by Pfizer $5.2B), Genocea Biosciences (NASDAQ: GNCA), Visterra (acquired for $430M), and recently Slack (NYSE: WORK), Guardant Health (NASDAQ: GH) and Singular Genomics (NASDAQ: OMIC).

Dr. Wong holds a Ph.D from Harvard in Engineering Sciences, a B.S in Chemical Engineering and B.S in Biology from MIT. During her time at Harvard, she created the world’s first inhaled TB vaccine. This training refined her skills in developing a structured approach to assessing opportunities and tackling complex challenges – a core component of recognizing and investing in innovative companies.

I spent more than two decades as a finance and business development executive in large, multi-national corporations. I got to work on some really cool things (like the transformation from delivering software via floppy disks to delivering it via the cloud) and some really big things (like a USD$7 BILLION acquisition). I love the advisory work I'm doing now but I'm still searching for the thing that's going to define my next chapter - something that is going to give me purpose and make me feel like I had a positive impact on the world. One of the areas I'm most passionate about is gender equality. Despite this being a discussion topic for decades, we've made little progress as a global community. 

If you also care about this topic, please do reach out. I'm open to ideas and supporting others!

impact_goals_nz:
Neither government nor philanthropy offer the complete solution to the environmental, social, and economic problems we face. I want New Zealand to show the world how smart entrepreneurs can use technology, innovation, and clever business models to build businesses that are sustainably profitable and inherently positive. 

Through Made Possible, I want to connect New Zealand businesses and technology to investors, funding, and international opportunities and - on the other side of the coin - connect investors to scalable, positive and profitable investment opportunities.  

Through The CleanCleaner Project, and other ventures and investments, I want to be leading by example demonstrating a model for positive impact and success.

five_things_to_know:
An advisor, founder, investor, and engineer, Mark is on a journey of impact, innovation and learning. With a love of the outdoors - our oceans, forests, and mountains - and a passion for technology and business, he's determined to direct this journey toward developing sustainable ventures that will ensure the environment and natural world is preserved for our children and future generations. 

Made Possible is an innovation and investment consultancy. We bridge the gap between technology and finance and help impact-oriented entrepreneurs and businesses develop strategy, articulate ideas, find partners and get the funding they need to make a difference.  

The CleanCleaner Project is a powerful opportunity to demonstrate success in a circular economy. We have developed some super effective - natural, all good, plant and mineral-based – home and personal care products which are sold as a powder that dissolves in water at home. This eliminates the environmental impact, cost, and inconvenience of manufacturing, shipping, storing, and disposing of bulky single-use containers filled mostly with water.

five_things_to_know:
1. I do not have accounts with anything Google or Facebook, and very strong views on why I go through lengths to not share my life with these surveillance capitalists. I use Free Software and open alternatives, such as the Firefox browser, and the Element messenger, and I am constantly trying to teach the people around me why they should take more care how they tread online.

2. I've been using Linux since 1994, and exclusively since 1998, and I'm a big proponent of Free Software and open standards. I operate a network of almost 50 Linux servers world-wide, which provide services to non-profits and individuals free of charge, which I use as my learning platform.

3. During the Iraq war in 2003, aljazeera.net ran off my servers for some weeks. I suspect this is the reason why I am on the US No-Fly list.

4. I came across Bitcoin in 2011 and was active all over the place in 2014, including being one of the first 10 investors in Ethereum. The after-events of the DAO "hack" in 2016 was a pinnacle moment for me though, and I've since grown completely disillusioned: blockchain is a hammer looking for a nail, either burning the planet, or recreating capitalism cryptographically. I have yet to see an actual problem solved by blockchain which could not be solved another way.

5. I burnt out at age 17 on track towards becoming a professional tennis player. I am now enjoying squash, and playing way too often for my body

Proudly the Founder of the world’s key HR search company with 100 employees with 1200 HR Leaders appointed to date across 60 countries in APAC, EMEA and Americas / www.chapmancg.com.

ChapmanCG was acquired by Will Group in 2019 and Matt sits as Chairman of ChapmanCG, managing the CEO and working with the Executive team, as well as on Will Group Executive Leadership team. He maintains close friendships and advisory with many of the world’s HR leaders.
five_things_to_know:
I am the Co-Owner of Bawah Reserve, is at the nexus of global Human Resources (via ChapmanCG) and Wellbeing (via ChapmanV). My ongoing personal formula is represented through my coaching brand MC2. My personal wellbeing journey is being chartered via Metamorphosis.

I founded ChapmanCG, the world’s main HR specific search firm, that out of Singapore and now has presence across Asia Pacific, Europe, Middle East Africa, North America and LATAM.

The vast HR alumni in the ChapmanCG network run the HR strategy of most of the world’s companies. Since 2008 ChapmanCG has appointed in excess of 2,000 HR leaders in 75 countries.

ChapmanCG secured major investment from Will Group, listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange, in early 2019. I am involved in Will’s strategy across nine acquisitions, including ChapmanCG, and sits as part of their leadership team.

I am the Co-creator of Thrive HR Exchange which focuses on a partnership with Thrive Global APAC and Monash University, growing the APAC HR community with wellbeing at the core.

I am the Founder of ChapmanV, a private knowledge and investment platform concentrating around wellness practices and wellness technologies. It aggregates my greatest passions around wellness for my global network of friends, alumni, HR network, business network and other interested parties.

I am the co-Founder of Risk Management Intelligence, an Asia Pacific background checking firm, based in Singapore and The Philippines.

I am an HR advisor for Cocoon Capital, in Singapore. I am also a board member of UltraX in the UK. I am an advisory member to BecomingX, co-founded by Bear Grylls, in the UK. I am also an International Liaison Director and Founding Member for the Snowman Race Committee in Bhutan.

I am an owner of Bawah Reserve in the Anambas Islands region of Indonesia, 160 nautical miles from Singapore, which Conde Nast magazine named in the top 10 of Asia’s luxury island destinations, in 2019. I also co-founded the Bawah Anambas Foundation.

I co-founded Automuse, in New Zealand.

I am the owner of Parihoa, in New Zealand, a multi faceted wellness, events and market place based around a beautiful farm. More concepts from Parihoa are coming this month.

I am a Singapore Citizen and Permanent Resident of New Zealand. I was born in the Hunter Valley, NSW in Australia.

I am an ultra-runner having completed 250km ultra runs in Atacama (Chile), Gobi (China), Sahara (Egypt), Antarctica, Namibia and Madagascar.

I am a Founder of the Snowman Run in Bhutan.

Launched and grew joyful.org in Australia and NZ to >$1m saas recurring revenue within a year.

Via snowballeffect.co.nz i am looking for and can help NZ growth companies that require capital.

I love advising and mentoring so am always available for an informal chat or a more formal board appointment.

impact_goals_nz: Develop the circular economy for packaging in the booming e-commerce sector, so that it's the standard and therefore it's a conscious choice not to use re-usable packaging.
how_to_support_my_work: I'm looking for advice and investment into my re-usable packaging as a service business for e-commerce, so am keen to chat to investors and those who can advise on this sector.
five_things_to_know:
I have experience in the commercial 'for-purpose' sector with two successful exits and also experience in the not for profit sector.  I'm currently learning all about the investment landscape in NZ and am working with the awesome folk at snowballeffect.co.nz.

Most recently I established, launched and grew joyful.org in Australia and NZ to >$1m SaaS recurring revenue within a year. Joyful was a subsidiary of RaisingIT.com, the UK's leading charity website and fundraising platform, until we were acquired in April 2019 by The Access Group.

Prior to Joyful, I founded Gnewt in 2009. Gnewt was a UK first - a totally zero emissions at point of use logistics business. It delivers and collects parcels and packages the 'final mile' within cities using a fleet of entirely electric vehicles. The fleet is over 100 strong and delivers upwards of 5 million items every year in the UK. Clients include other parcel carriers, businesses and retailers. Gnewt launched in November 2009 and was acquired in August 2017 by Menzies Distribution, then part of John Menzies Plc.

I also have experience in the not-for-profit sector having co-founded (and am now an advisor to) BlueVentures.org (BV) in 2002, an organisation with operations in Madagascar, Belize and Timor-Leste that develops transformative approaches for catalysing and sustaining locally led marine conservation. BV works in places where the ocean is vital to local cultures and economies, and is committed to protecting marine biodiversity in ways that benefit coastal people.

Currently I'm developing On a Mission, which seeks to bring best in class technology to the logistics sector in NZ/AU creating awesome customer experiences both for retailers and consumers, as well as ensuring sustainability and the environment is front and centre. To combat the single use packaging issue in e-commerce, we're currently developing a re-usable packaging as a service concept for e-retailers, businesses and individuals (boop.co.nz).

career_highlights:
AimHi Earth - Co-founder, Chair & Chief Scientist
☉ Racing to activate millions of nature-first thinkers, to bring about a future where nature has a seat at every boardroom, political and kitchen table.
☉ Supported by leading lights like Sir Jonathon Porritt, Chris Packham CBE, Dr Cécile Girardin, Paul Hawken, Sir Tim Smit, Lord Jim Knight and Sabra Noordeen.
☉ Changed the direction of companies, the kits of football players, the speeches of Olympic medallists, the funding strategy of charities and the output of broadcasters.
☉ >95% audience retention, >50% per-minute engagement, >90% measurable behaviour change (https://www.aimhi.earth/our-impact)

Science Communicator
☉ I like communicating science, I always have.
☉ I like sharing things that are beautiful and astonishing, and I love being able to help others to understand, to feel empowered, and to be curious too.
☉ My videos online have been viewed tens of millions of times and featured in VICE, and my popular science podcast is supported by QI co-founder, John Mitchinson.
☉ I once gave a TEDx talk from a bathtub, and over the years, I've been lucky to be invited onto Blue Peter, BBC’s Today programme, Sky News, Piers Morgan and Comedy Central.
☉ I've also spoken at a number of events, from company/charity AGMs, conferences and chambers of commerce, to protest rallies, festivals and UN events.

Earthly Institute - Non-Executive Director
☉ The Earthly Institute exists to bridge the gap between the law and science through education, advocacy and research.
☉ By engaging over 100 million people a year via social media, the Earthly Institute is one of the world's most effective accelerators of attention towards (primarily legal) environmental campaigns.

Global CO2 Widget - Founder & Coordinator
☉ The global level of carbon dioxide and its rate of increase are two of the most important factors in deciding the future of our civilisation and life on Earth.
☉ This widget exists to make these two ideas readily available to anyone in the world.
☉ It's already the go-to widget for national broadcasters from Canada to Japan.
☉ Planned and coordinated with support from Cambridge Zero / University of Cambridge, the widget is embeddable, compact, colourful, easy to understand and free.

#NoBeef - Co-founder & Coordinator
☉ The evidence is clear – humans and organisations are much quicker to make small, logical changes (like dropping beef and lamb) than large ones (reducing all animal products).
☉ However, once a small change is made and emotionally “owned”, it snowballs.
☉ This is why so many people and organisations have gone via #NoBeef to vegetarianism and even plant-based diets. It may not be the direct route, but it’s frequently the fastest.
☉ The campaign has had support from the likes of Peter Gabriel, Paul McCartney, the University of Cambridge, Goldsmiths University, David Katz and Peter Singer.
☉ Campaign video: https://www.facebook.com/watch/?v=2199905820028000

Planet A - Co-founder & Coordinator
☉ Among other campaigns, we ran #ReforestEarth. We built a temporary forest of 1000 trees outside the UK parliament, involving hundreds of members of parliament (MPs) gifting every MP a sapling whilst clearly communicating the science and urgency of rewilding.
☉ A month later, the Conservative party agreed to a 100-fold increase in their reforestation plans (from 11M to 1B trees).
☉ Many UK members of parliament still have these trees growing in their gardens or constituencies.

Ash Lad - Musician
☉ In the distant past, I made music. My co-creator, Sam Lea, and I had support from Radio 1, NME, the Guardian, and we were booked for festivals like Glastonbury and Latitude.

Other advisory / board roles:
☉ ClimateScience
☉ Purpose PBC
☉ World Congress of Science & Factual Producers

five_things_to_know:
Maxine Burkett is a Professor of Law at the William S. Richardson School of Law, University of Hawai‘i and a Global Fellow at the Woodrow Wilson International Center for Scholars. She is also a Co-Founder and Senior Advisor to the non-profit Institute for Climate and Peace. Maxine is an expert in the law and policy of climate change, with a specific focus on climate justice, climate-induced migration, and climate change, peace, and conflict.  Her work has been cited in numerous news and policy outlets, including BBC Radio, the New York Times, the Washington Post, and Nature Climate Change. From 2009-2012, Maxine also served as the inaugural director of the Center for Island Climate Adaptation and Policy. Maxine received her B.A. from Williams College and Exeter College, Oxford University, and received her J.D. from the University of California, Berkeley.

She serves on the boards of Blue Planet Foundation, The Climate Museum, ELAW, and Global Greengrants Fund, and was a member of the Federal Advisory Committee for the Sustained National Climate Assessment. Maxine is also a member scholar of the Center for Progressive Reform, the Lancet Commission for Reparations and Redistributive Justice, and the American Law Institute.

career_highlights: Part of the Ceres Robotics team, focuses on surface robotics and planetary surface landers. In 2019 they were selected by Orbit Beyond to develop and deliver the first commercial lunar surface robot on the lunar lander mission. Whilst that mission was then cancelled, Ceres were later selected to join the preferred lunar lander providers at NASA.
how_im_looking_to_support_the_nz_ecosyst: I look forward to mentoring in the areas of opportunities and technologies relevant to space exploration. Space exploration is fundamentally a human endeavor that belongs to us all. Similar to the Pacific Islanders extending frontiers across the Pacific, today our exploration of the solar system expands the frontiers for everyone and especially importantly for all our youth.
key_networks:
SpaceBase.co - Kiwi based international entrepreneurial space support network.
International Space community.
impact_goals_nz: We plan to build our New Zealand component of Ceres to share the vision and the work of humanity's exploration of space. We expect our initial focus will be on virtual reality modeling. Our initial feeling is that is a good fit between our needs as a space companies and strengths of New Zealand. However, we also intend to be adaptive to opportunities and not to be afraid to pivot.
how_to_support_my_work:
In addition to supporting our Ceres Robotics effort, one can also connect with our Kiwi partners, SpaceBase (spacebase.co), and Extraterrestrial Power (extraterrestrialpower.com). 

Our intended focus in EHF will be on space virtual reality modeling for use in actual missions, so contacts with New Zealand VR players is appreciated. In addition we view space exploration as an endeavor of all of humanity, and we view VR and visualizations are part of sharing the excitement and expansion of what is possible.
five_things_to_know: I come from AI and Robotics applied to space missions, and looking forward I see the positive implications of that for all of humanity. To bring us together in passion, in being enriched by our differences, and in playing a part in paving a path toward a future we want. At Ceres Robotics our mission is to build planetary surface landers and robots that make it affordable for humans to become interplanetary. Both for technical reasons in missions and for sharing the experience we use virtual reality. It is our plan to build our Ceres virtual reality systems in New Zealand.

career_highlights:
Driven by business excellence and social change, my personal mission is to create a more inclusive society, one in which no one is left behind.
In December 2020, I was appointed CEO of UNICEF Aotearoa New Zealand, one of 33 UNICEF National Committees, which raise funds for UNICEF’s worldwide emergency and development work. 

Prior to this appointment, I was drawn to the role of Chief Executive Officer at Kilmarnock Enterprises; a pioneering social enterprise providing education, employment and opportunities for people with learning disabilities. For over 11 years, my passion for the nonprofit sector enabled me to use commercial excellence as a tool to demonstrate the value of people with intellectual disabilities to our customers and the community.

During my career, I have also spent over one and a half decades working in the Telecommunications sector, both in the UK and Aotearoa - most recently as Vodafone’s Head of Digital Innovation and Head of South Island.
I have worked tirelessly to increase diversity in the workplace and leadership roles. I view overcoming barriers as an opportunity to drive change and strongly believe in helping those who sit on the margins of society. 
I have also had the privilege of holding multiple directorships including that of Kilmarnock, Nutrient Rescue and the Akina Foundation. I am also a proud Edmund Hillary Fellow and Independent Chair of BNZ. 
Diversity and inclusion have become absolutely core to everything I do, I am passionate about creating transformative impact, for people, planet and profit.

Strategic Thinker
Includer
Passionate and Positive
Activator
Change Agent

Co-founder of 2BitCircus, a groundbreaking entertainment company that uses innovative technologies to blur the lines between physical and digital playgrounds. Amoung other first, Nancy created the first episodic sport content in VR for Google and the NFL.
how_im_looking_to_support_the_nz_ecosyst: Mentorship matters to our team.  All three of us have loads of experience to share. We seek connection with health care researchers and workers who have experience with visually challenged Kiwi residents, particularly in working with Maori as well as with those who have regulatory expertise in navigating the New Zealand health care system. We will need to understand regulations around employment in the country.
key_networks:
Sundance New Frontier Labs Program Grantee
British Academy of Film and Television Arts
Producers Guild of America
National Academy of Recording Arts and Sciences (Grammys)
Founder, Advisor Two Bit Circus, Inc.
Founder, Board Member Bitmax.net (Bitmax, inc)
impact_goals_nz: Our EHF team (Dr. J. Bennett, J. van Dijk and myself) is eager to improve outcomes for patients through the development and adoption of a novel virtual reality test of functional vision. The virtual reality outcome measure that we have developed (the Multi-luminance orientation and Mobility Test, MLoMT) is already the primary outcome measure for an exciting gene therapy clinical trial targeting a congenital blindness (https://ir.opusgtx.com/press-releases/detail/474/opus-genetics-announces-updates-on-opgx-lca5-clinical-program). our team intends to further expand the virtual reality outcome measure in NZ so that it can be used to validate positive results in other interventions (gene therapy, cell therapy and small molecule) that are being developed. We also plan to set the stage so that some of these interventional studies can take place in NZ. While it has taken a couple of years to get our studies launched in NZ, we have used that time to make sure that they will not only move quickly but will also be successful. Our goal is ultimately to ameliorate vision-robbing disorders so that this will benefit individuals, families, society, and the healthcare system in NZ. We predict that this venture will not only improve the lives of many New Zealanders suffering from sight-robbing conditions, but will also have a global impact.
how_to_support_my_work: We will be initiating a raise and would welcome connection with VCs who have serious interest in AI and advanced digital diagnostic tools.
five_things_to_know:
Nancy Bennett is an innovative storyteller and visionary creative who thrives at the nexus of groundbreaking technologies, cinematic storytelling, and creative design. With 20+ years of experience spanning VR, film, television, music videos, marketing, and startups, she expertly leads cross-functional teams to bring trailblazing ideas to life across physical and digital platforms. Nancy’s creative background, hands-on technical expertise, and futurist vision enable her to shape ultra-modern experiences that drive revenue growth, engage audiences by the millions, and transform the possibilities of tomorrow into the reality of today. Bennett began her career in Music Videos and commercials, working on seminal hits for the Beastie Boys, En Vogue, Tracy Chapman and the B52s to name a few. She was simultaneously Head of Production for the home video arm, the audio books division and the music video department of Atlantic Records and started the new media division between 1990-1994. She directed and produced hit videos for Tori Amos, Led Zeppelin, Mr Big and Ringo Starr from 1994-98. In 1998, Bennett founded the now 23-year-old transcoding entity, bitmax.net handling media for Universal Music worldwide, Apple, ATT and other entertainment studios today. In 2004, she designed and launched LOGO, the first LGBTQ TV network in history for MTV Networks. She launched RockBand II as creative marketing lead in 2008. She directed and activated the first out-of-home VR experience for 20,000 NFL fans during Super Bowl XLIV in 2015. She directed the first episodic series in VR for the NFL and Google in 2016. Bennett is one of the five founding members of Two Bit Circus, an interdisciplinary team of technologists and artists where she served as Chief Creative Officer for the company and launched the first ever micro amusement park of immersive entertainment. Bennett is a recipient of the 2018 Lumiere for Best Music VR and a 2019 Sundance Institute New Frontier Lab Programs Grantee. Bennett has a performance French horn degree and a Master of Science in film. Nancy mentors Techstars start-ups focused on technology, immersive entertainment and music.
 
Patent PCT/ /US2019/029173 for METHODS, SYSTEMS, AND COMPUTER READABLE MEDIA FOR TESTING VISUAL FUNCTION USING VIRTUAL MOBILITY TESTS Bennett, J, Aleman, TS, Ashtari, M, Hamilton, A, Bennett, N., Design and methodology for testing visual function using simulated living situations in individuals with visual disorders; Penn Docket 18-8557; PCT/US2019/029173; WO 2019/210087 A1; priority date 25 April 2018

thought partnership on alternative ownership and finance structures to drive greater impact, connections to resources that can leverage change

key_networks:
Recent EHF Collaboration:  whitepaper "Purpose Driven Structures for Impact Entrepreneurs in New Zealand" 

Purpose Economy
impact_goals_nz:
I am looking to learn from and contribute to new ways of thinking about ownership and transformative finance in Aotearoa New Zealand.

I am currently working with entrepreneur and investor fellows on several ventures that embrace stewardship-ownership principles, innovating design using existing NZ legal forms.

Recently organized an EHF collaboration with a team of top lawyers to published a whitepaper applying the concepts and legal forms of stewardship ownership in NZ, aimed at providing inspiration and practical tools/advice:
https://chapmantripp.com/trends-insights/purpose-driven-structures-for-impact-entrepreneurs-in-aotearoa-new-zealand/
five_things_to_know:
I work with purpose-driven entrepreneurs and investors to innovate new ownership and investment models that lock-in purpose, create broader shared prosperity, value nature, enhance business resilience and success.

I have spent the last two decades as an executive and change maker working to make the food system healthier, more just and ecologically aligned. In recent years, I have turned my focus to transformative finance and steward ownership models.  

In 2019, I co-founded Alternative Ownership Advisors, we help purpose-driven companies and investors rethink structures and terms for better outcomes. I also serve as a Trustee of the Sustainable Food & Agriculture Purpose Trust, that owns independently managed and sustainability-rooted food companies, providing liquidity and transition for original owners or growth funding, while protecting their mission and ensuring a focus on serving stakeholders.

I was a Vice President at Organically Grown Company, one of the largest independent distributors of organic produce in the US, where in 2018 I led a move to restructure the company ownership under a Perpetual Purpose Trust—pioneering an innovative use of a new model to hold company ownership to enshrine long-term mission-focus, raise impact capital, share governance and returns with the stakeholder community.

I believe the planet and society face critical challenges, part of the root of the problem is shareholder primacy paradigm. In order to fix a broken system we need to  rethink fundamentals and build new models. Business can be a powerful force for change, if we look at how to retool the goals and incentives to drive different, more holistic outcomes. Indigenous wisdom, movements around diversity, equity and inclusion, and environmental justice can inform new ways of thinking.

name: Oonagh Browne
bio_tagline: To transform the chocolate industry, Cacao farmers lives in the Pacific and mental wellness through the power of authentic cacao.
currently_based_in: Christchurch, Canterbury 8011 New Zealand
search_tags: The Cacao Ambassador, Oct22
global_networks: American Samoa, Australia, New Zealand, Solomon Islands
industries_other: Documentary
career_highlights: Co-founder of an award winning chocolate company, Master Chocolatier and Cacao expert. Founded The Cacao Ambassador to focus on Cacao Education, from public workshops, talks, demos and team building, to on the ground real transformative work in Cacao growing communities, to transform the chocolate industry. Pitching and winning contracts with AID organisations to fund the work I am passionate about.
how_im_looking_to_support_the_nz_ecosyst:
Having lived in NZ for 16 years, I am available for an advice, support, mentoring in how to hit the ground running here, as its an environment for the possibility of rapid support growth. Also if any or everything of the above vision connects with your work, to co-create and work together in any way would be fantastic.

I am an open door and love the power of synergy for the greatest impact and outcome!
impact_goals_nz:
1) To build an entirely new Chocolate industry, putting New Zealand on the global map for being the first and the best in making volume couverture chocolate, that is sustainable, ethical, fully traceable (like never before), and for the first time ever is fully bean to bar, and has the health benefits listed, tested and proven. This will reduce NZ chocolate imports of couverture and compete on a global level in global couverture chocolate. To make the way of this transparent so other chocolate companies can learn the way and the chocolate industry starts to change. 

2) To work on the ground with our local neighbours who grow Cacao, the Pacific Islands, and inspire, empower and change the current dynamics where their lives are not truly supported by their Cacao farms. This starts with their own connection with the crop in understanding how to use it, that growing into their skills in growing fine Cacao and finally how they can run a business in their way, maintaining the qualities of their culture, but in a way that gives back to their family, their farm and changes their lives for the better.

3) Put Cacao on the mainstream stage for mental wellness in New Zealand. This includes robust research, mainstream health organisations promoting it and it being available in therapy dosage throughout the country.

4) Educating the New Zealand consumers on Cacao and how to use it as a daily food alongside chocolate.

5) Production of a documentary that tells the story and the wisdom of Cacao in a modern way, and inspires global audiences on how it truly is an ancient medicine for these modern times. 

6) The writing of cookbooks in how to use Cacao as a daily food alongside chocolate, for the countries who grow Cacao and consumers who enjoy it alike.
how_to_support_my_work:
1) Support in opening doors for robust scientific testing of Cacao
2) Support with upscaling, R&D and readiness for export
3) Opening doors for the pitching of the documentary
4) Opening doors into the mental wellness arena
five_things_to_know:
I left the corporate world in my late 20's, as it was soulless and traveled for a few years, moved to Australia and started to make one chocolate and a love affair like no other erupted in the art and craft of fine chocolate! This blossomed into co-founding one of New Zealand's finest artisanal chocolate companies, (moved to New Zealand 16 years ago), winning many awards. However, the journey continued into the healing power of Cacao, when after the Christchurch earthquakes, I had a breakdown and refused to go onto antidepressant, and instead started to explore natural medicines and discovered the power of cacao with other super-foods. Whilst resting and recovering on a friend's Cacao farm in Samoa, I first heard the Cacao trees speak to me and reveal my true destiny, which is to represent the voice of Cacao, and its potent medicine and to take it to the world as a key tool for supporting in these transformative times, especially for Mental Wellness. Since then, I have journeyed to the origin of Cacao where I developed a global vision for what is possible in how to change the chocolate industry and for the past 3 years, have worked with the Solomon Islands and recently PNG, in changing, for the better, their story with Cacao. They now for the first time, export boutique Cacao, and after 60 years of growing it have started to use it in their local cooking and even making their own chocolate in the heart of the villages. The work has included training on farm management, sustainability, village banking, cook book writing and developing a prototype model that is applicable in Cacao farming communities across the globe. This is just the beginning and the vision can be summarised in the following three pillars for change:

To radically change the chocolate industry in returning to the true source of the ingredient Cacao and building three strong pillars:

1) Working on the ground with the Cacao farmers and communities

2) Leading the way in the chocolate industry in what true ethical sourcing and chocolate making can be, and leading this from New Zealand to the world, becoming a key exporter and making the way transparent for other chocolate companies.

3) Educating the consumer on the power of Cacao as a medicine, especially for mental Wellness and empowering them to make real choices in what they purchase in chocolate, and cacao.

Rua Manuel Matos Mindelo,  Cabo Verde
65 Valkenburgerstraat Amsterdam, Noord-Holland 1011 MG Nederland
search_tags: human rights film, documentary film, creative documentary, injustice, climate change, indigenous wisdom, indigenous knowledge, interconnection, Arts, Education, Human Rights, Indigenous Knowledge, Entrepreneur
ventures: I AM THE RIVER, THE RIVER IS ME, Myanmar Diaries, ZINDOC
industries_other: documentary film production, impact storytelling
career_highlights:
Ph.D. from Harvard in political philosophy, and professor at the EUI Florence and CEU Budapest before leaving academia to become an independent filmmaker in 2003 specialising in human rights film. 

His films have premiered at Sundance, the Berlinale, Venice, IDFA and Rotterdam, have screened at over 450 festivals around the world and have been distributed and broadcast in more than 30 countries. 

Since 2013, he has invested a great deal of energy in working in Myanmar. First teaching human rights films, then releasing a creative documentary filmed over four years - Burma Storybook (2016) - a film about Myanmar's democratic transition told through the eyes of its dissident poets. In 2022 he released Myanmar Diaries - a hybrid documentary film about life under the regime of terror in Myanmar in the aftermath of its military coup of February 1st 2021, told through personal stories by a group of anonymous young Burmese filmmakers. This film won the Oscar-qualifying 2022 Berlinale Award as well as fourteen other international prizes to date. 

In 2024 he released I AM THE RIVER, THE RIVER IS ME, a film about Aotearoa New Zealand's Whanganui River, the first river in the world recognised by law as a legal person, and as an indivisible and living spiritual being.  Made in close collaboration with the Whanganui Iwi over a period of three years, the film is now being released around the globe. 

This year, he will be soon releasing THE CORIOLIS EFECT, a creative poetic documentary about our world spinning out of control, set in Cape Verde - the place where hurricanes are born. 

He works together with his partner and producer Corinne van Egeraat who is also an EHF fellow. 

They see it as their kaupapa to dedicate their energies to making films about the rights of nature.
key_networks: IDFA, Berlinale, Sundance, Venice, Doc Edge, Movies that Matter, the Hague.
impact_goals_nz:
Our goals in NZ:

1. To develop the talent of young indigenous filmmakers in NZ and Oceania.
2. To help promote Māori wisdom about nature globally (and we say this with deep humility and with the awareness that our first task is to listen and learn, to become serious students of Māori wisdom ourselves.)
3. To help bring about a value shift in attitudes/perspectives on nature inspired by Māori wisdom.
4. To offer our storytelling and creative advice to all EHF fellows.
how_to_support_my_work:
We are looking for partners who could help us leverage the impact from our film WE ARE THE RIVER: to help bring about a global attitude and perceptual shift inspired by indigenous wisdom to live in harmony with nature.

We want to connect with experts in any discipline working on the subject of interconnectivity to help us generate impact.
five_things_to_know:
I left my academic career - I have a PhD from Harvard in political theory - in 2004 to become a self-taught independent documentary filmmaker specialised in human rights film. My work has premiered at the most prestigious international film festivals - Sundance, Berlin, Venice, IDFA and Rotterdam, has screened at over 350 festivals around the world and has been broadcast in over 20 countries. 

I am passionate about making films about injustice, and about breaking down barriers and borders through storytelling. 

I make my films together with my partner Corinne van Egeraat - who is also an EHF Fellow.

Three of our most recent productions:

Myanmar Diaries (2022).  A hybrid feature documentary about life under the regime of terror in Myanmar in the aftermath of its military coup of February 1st 2021, told through personal stories by a group of anonymous young Burmese filmmakers. Winner of the Oscar-qualifying Berlinale Documentary Award and 14 other international awards to date as it continues its international roll out. 

Angels on Diamond Street (2019). A documentary about three inspiring women fighting for social justice in an African American church in North Philadelphia. One day, an undocumented Mexican mother asks the church for sanctuary...

Burma Storybook (2016). A four-year long project in Myanmar about the country's transition from dictatorship told through the eyes of dissident poets. It culminated with an award-winning feature documentary screened around the world and in free impact screenings throughout Myanmar. We also produced a book of photography and poetry with photographs by internationally acclaimed photographer Dana Lixenberg. And we taught and mentored young human rights filmmakers and documentary photographers in Myanmar.

We are currently in production on THE CORIOLIS EFFECT - an ambitious creative documentary feature film about how we are all at risk of losing our global home in a world spinning out of control, set in Cape Verde. We hope the film is a call to action to respect the natural world. 
 
We have also spent the last three years developing WE ARE THE RIVER, our first venture in New Zealand. And in these last three months, we have just completed production shooting of the film. We will begin postproduction in April 2023.  This will be a  feature documentary about New Zealand's Whanganui River - the first river in the world to be granted legal personhood, and to be recognised as a spiritual, living entity.

We'd like to make a film that communicates, through cinematic storytelling, the Māori indigenous world-view of the interconnectedness all things. 

Our hoped for impact: to help bring about a global shift in relating to nature inspired by indigenous wisdom and reverence for the natural world.
anything_else_to_share:
--Slack response time: Occasionally. 
--Interest in Māori values or culture: perspectives on nature, cosmology, spirituality. 
--Working with Māori communities?: Yes. 
--Key NZ/EHF connections: We are well connected to the DOC EDGE documentary festival - we have screened two films with them in the past and have pitched at their forum.. 
--Reason joined EHF: Would like to learn about Maori values and spirituality. And help create impact with indigenous wisdom about nature: the idea that all is connected, that we are part of a greater sacred whole, and that we have a responsibility to nature to live in harmony and balance with it. I see this as a fundamental way to tackle climate change.  And I am thrilled to be part of an idealistic community of extraordinary people who want to change the world!. 
--Energy to connect in person (10 is max): 9. 
--Energy to connect virtually (10 is max): 5.

five_things_to_know:
CEO and Co-founder of Memori, a holistic digital legacy planning platform that provides affordable and accessible legacy planning solutions. It is a first of its kind campaign in Brunei to demystify and democratize the process, costs, and choices of legacy planning, regardless of race or religion. Memori aims to offer sustainable solutions for Asian funeral rites, which unfortunately involve a lot of open burning. Memori is working on low-cost, environmentally-friendly funeral biodegradable options for the masses and underprivileged. Memori’s work has impacted several communities, bagging several Global Startup awards and being featured in several international media.

impact_goals_nz:
Committed to supporting and leading positive impact in my local community and in Aotearoa NZ.

We are in a dynamic and challenging time with accelerating climate impacts, civil unrest and impatience with the current economic and governance systems, massive inequity and displacement of people globally. A biodiversity crisis and the anthropocene (a small word for something so profound) demanding we pay attention.

I believe we need to have incredibly brave, creative, bold people around decision making tables, and leading and nurturing innovation and transformative change across all areas and sectors of our communities - locally, nationally, globally - and in these roles, we need to connect, collaborate and create better outcomes for all.
five_things_to_know:
I spent my early 20s sailing all over the world, at the frontline of environmental advocacy, education and activism.

Subsequently I co-founded a social enterprise focused on youth development - leadership, sustainability education and global citizenship - with a significant international focus. 

After two decades in that space, I stepped away, to focus on professional governance. Now serving in private and public sector governance roles and actively working on supporting and leading change in my local community and in Aotearoa NZ, as we transition to a socially just and environmentally sustainable future.

I take the role of treaty partnership, being a kaitiaki/guardian and being a good ancestor seriously. Currently working in partnership on the ecological restoration of Te Taonui-a-Kupe (Cape Jackson, 650ha peninsula in the Marlborough Sounds) - including carbon offsetting/drawdown through indigenous forest regeneration.

five_things_to_know:
Raphael is CEO and Founder of Amy, an Artificial Intelligence-based private tutoring system for maths. He is passionate about the future of education and ensuring that everyone in the world has equal access. He is actively involved in the development of the startup ecosystem and helped to establish Canterbury Angel Investors and mentors at Startup Weekends around the world. He received a global leadership award at the World Education Congress in 2017 and Amy was a finalist in the EduTech Asia best startup award for 2019.

Amy is an AI-based private math tutor that seamlessly integrates into all online learning management systems. Amy makes learning maths easy by giving students feedback and automatically filling their knowledge gaps in real time. She has a fundamental understanding of math and uses this to teach just like a human tutor. Dynamic teaching, as we call this, is a paradigm shift on the current model of adaptive learning. Amy also provides teachers the data they need to be more effective and saves them a lot of time. She takes care of the mundane aspects of teaching so they can focus on the human part of their job and inspire students to learn at their full potential.

Our vision is to democratise education globally and ensure that everyone with a basic internet connection can have access to high quality education.

Dr Reza Jarral is a practising primary care physician, Edmund Hillary Fellow and  'AI4Health' advisor at the World Health Organisation. Prior to joining CareHQ as the chief medical officer for virtual health, Reza has worked alongside marginalised communities for over a decade, delivering care at the front line in the NHS, New Zealand and sub-Saharan Africa. In 2022 Reza was appointed the clinical director for equity at ProCare, New Zealand's largest primary healthcare organisation. Reza guides investment in deep health technology at Bridgewest Ventures, helping local startups with great ideas to scale globally.

Reza's multilateral work on the impact of responsible, sustainable business practice on human well-being has been formally recognised by The Rockefeller Foundation, Microsoft 'AI for Good' and Acumen. In 2021 Reza was named an Emerging Alumni Leader by Imperial College London. 

Reza relishes collaboration and looks forward to shaping a brighter, better, collective future using technology.

I am open to explore the best in digital innovation and responsible design with impact-driven organisations. 

I'm passionate about the intersection of technology and society. I practice in this space with the WHO as an expert clinical advisor, ITU, IEEE and AIMed. I lecture on AI, ethics and society at Tech Futures Lab, Auckland, and peer review for the Royal College of Surgeons Journal. 

I've been fortunate to work with wonderful people on exciting projects utilising telemedicine, AI, social innovation and decentralised workforces. As a senior clinician with over 10,000 consultations spanning both hemispheres I remain primarily a people person; centred on achieving the very best in personal and population health.

I am hopeful that I can continue my work in preventative mental health care and education for a long time in both NZ and the US.  In late 2024, I will very likely start a small technical business in NZ.
five_things_to_know:
I contribute to a variety of projects by writing, producing, analyzing, researching and designing systems.  I am also an angel investor who takes a very active utility role in most businesses I invest in, oftentimes with charitable organizations.

I have worn many hats in startups, across a variety of industries, from farming, to digital ledger tech, to aerospace, to education.  Almost all of those businesses were purpose-driven.  

Along the way, I was lucky enough to build some of the first DLT-based projects for purpose-driven organizations like the World Food Program and Mozilla.  As far as my interests in decentralization, I have since that time been experimenting with systems-level decentralization and communications.

I have worked on the ground with preventative mental health care practitioners, writing technical manuals, producing videos, and learning the work of researchers in that field.  I still support some organizations in those fields.

name: Ron Hicks
bio_tagline: Inventor, entrepreneur, and innovator. Bringing transparency to the protein industry.
currently_based_in:
3625 California St San Diego, California 92101 United States
503 S Main St Boerne, Texas 78006 United States
career_highlights: Founder and CEO of HerdX, which services the global retail food market by providing a transparency blockchain data network for live proteins that are raised properly, removing the need for antibiotics and other medications whilst reducing diseases. Began his career in the medical technology industry by inventing a vaccum-assisted negative pressure wound closure device, acquired by Kinetic Concepts Inc (KCI), subsequently bought by 3M.
how_im_looking_to_support_the_nz_ecosyst:
Our main goal is to integrate our technology with  NZ, bringing effective solutions to their farms to help them provide transparency to the consumers. We know many farmers have a story behind their incredible products and part of our service is helping communicate that to the consumer. We are also providing opportunities for companies like SnapIT, a Nelson, NZ based corporation, to expand their mission. 

See How They Fish; https://www.herdx.com/news/herdx-partners-with-snapit-to-begin-tracking-fish/
key_networks: American Chamber of Commerce New Zealand, North American Meat Institute, Greater Boerne Chamber of Commerce, Texas Farm Bureau
impact_goals_nz:
Now, more than ever, consumers are questioning where their food comes from. Farmers are working long hard hours with very tight margins. At HerdX, we are determined to bring transparency to the food supply chain and trust to the consumer, helping bridge the gap. 

We believe the technological solutions we provide will increase the livelihood of New Zealand farmers, boost the agricultural economy, and preserve the environments they depend on. Our goal is set; as consumers demand higher quality products and transparency, HerdX will deploy new tools to meet the demands with New Zealand food products and help deliver unique data to retailers, authenticating the products they want to sell. HerdX will help New Zealand spearhead this pivot in adopting best practices, positioning its farmers as leaders in the market.

HerdX will help build accurate supply chain transparency and bring to the forefront the need to raise New Zealand livestock following best practices to ensure the sustainability of the animals, the ranchers, and our planet. A big part of this is about sharing the rancher's story to revitalize the appreciation for agriculture and the food being grown. In the next 5 years, HerdX will continue the work of connecting the vast, independent network of millions of farmers, fishermen, corporations, and consumers each day. 

See How it Works; https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Q9I9FS8525U
how_to_support_my_work: We know the NZ community is strong and we want to serve it well. We are looking to further integrate with other cattle and sheep farmers to help bring transparency to their farms. The best way to support our impact is through connecting our service to farmers, spreading the important message that consumers are demanding transparency and we want to help farmers supply that need. Not only will the consumers benefit, but our product will also raise the value of NZ farmers' sheep, cattle, and other products. Overall, fueling the NZ economy, helping farmers, and bringing transparency to the consumer all through our innovative and data-driven solution.
five_things_to_know: Founder of HerdX, Inc., a software and technology company in the agriculture industry. Ron is an entrepreneur, an inventor, and a visionary with his sights set on helping farmers and ranchers have the use of technologies that are inspired by nature and authenticated with data for building Trust through Transparency. With a variety of immensely successful career paths and achievements, he was distinguished with Business Week’s top industrial design award which recognized him along with other company leaders throughout the world, including BMW, Sony, and Logitech. Governor Rick Perry of Texas chose Ron as a keynote speaker at the conference for Technology in Rural America to help advance agriculture to a new level of understanding becoming the foundation for the HerdX vision.

Since 1996, Ron has invested and managed over US$1.5 billion in real estate, energy and technology investments; raised seed rounds and institutional capital; run start-ups, turnarounds and established companies. 

Ron was previously the founder/CEO of Greenlots (acquired by Shell), Walz Group (acquired by LenderLive) and Colony Capital Japan (listed NYSE: DBRG). 

Ron graduated with honors from Berkeley where he founded the Credit Union for Berkeley Students (CUBS).
impact_goals_nz:
Where are schools for the real world? This is my passion and focus.

Education should prepare young people for jobs that do not yet exist, using technologies that have not yet been invented, to solve problems of which we are not yet aware. — Richard Riley (former US Secretary of Education).

Education should be relevant and impart practical skills for the real world.

Learning should be challenging and inspirational, incorporating a personalized approach focused on a student’s interests and strengths.

With accelerated progress in technology, education should provide exposure at an early age to new ways of thinking, the latest innovations, and collaboration with the real world.

Strong communication and interpersonal skills are essential to nurture.

Exposure to modern-day heroes, leaders and domain experts helps to improve motivation among students and provide a model for successful behavior.

Learning an ethical framework to help guide action is fundamental.
five_things_to_know:
Built two ventures that were acquired for $50m+ (Greenlots, Walz Group) with over 500 employees. 

Now focused on education (building), clean energy, technology and wellness (investing, advising).

name: Ryan Petersen
bio_tagline: I'm the founder of Flexport, a technology platform for global trade. We make it easy and affordable for businesses and social enterprises to ship freight anywhere in the world.
currently_based_in: 189 Van Ness Ave San Francisco, California 94102 United States
career_highlights: Founder and CEO of Flexport, a freight forwarding company, which delivers deep visibility and control, low and predictable supply chain costs, with faster and more reliable transit times, employs 2,000 employees in 25 locations. Ryan also created Flexport.org, which has provided international logistics services for millions of people affected by natural disasters and humanitarian relief work, including 62 million pieces of PPE during the COVID outbreak. He is also the Founder of Room.com, ImportGenius, BuildZoom, raising significant values of VC funds.
how_im_looking_to_support_the_nz_ecosyst: I've spent 20 years learning the art of entrepreneurship and trying to develop it into more of a science. I am passionate about helping entrepreneurs who've reached product-market fit to develop their company into an institution, an organization that is much bigger than themselves, and that can endure the test of time.
key_networks: Y Combinator
impact_goals_nz: I want to make it easier than ever for any entrepreneur in New Zealand, whether for profit or social enterprise,  to ship products to their customers and from their suppliers all over the world.  I'd like to see New Zealand emerge as much more than a source of raw materials, but to allow the immense human capital of the country to reach its potential, ensuring that the country's geographic distance from the rest of the world does not mean it cannot be an important hub for high-end manufacturing and creative product development.
how_to_support_my_work: Serendipity has its ways. In the meantime, I am eager to learn about the country, its culture, and the business ecosystem to see where I can be most helpful.
five_things_to_know:
At Flexport, our mission is to make global trade easy for everybody. We want to use technology to unite humanity in a seamless web of commerce, allowing any organization on the planet to buy or sell any product, in any market around the world. Every time two parties trade with one another, both are made better off, and humanity advances just a little bit. We are accelerating this progress by removing all the friction that makes it so hard for people to buy or sell goods internationally.

I believe that global trade is a force for good. In the last century, probably no technology had a bigger impact on global poverty than the shipping container. Export-lead growth in Asia lifted more than 1 billion people out of poverty in the last 50 years. Yet we kind of stopped there, and the industry hasn't innovated much. We're determined to use the power of technology to drive out all the inefficiencies that still remain in our global trade networks.

To help unlock bold, overlooked solutions to New Zealand’s toughest challenges—especially where small, catalytic funding or early backing can shift stuck systems. My goal is to work alongside other funders, backers, and ecosystem builders to:

Identify and support ventures or ideas with outsized potential that are underfunded, under-recognised, or ahead of their time

Co-create tools, models, and system maps that help more of us give boldly, learn fast, and back what truly matters

Prior to joining QEDIT, she was named a Curator, Global Shaper, and Media Leader for the World Economic Forum, and served as the Director of Special Projects at TechCrunch.

five_things_to_know:
Her award-winning art, shown recently during Art Basel Miami 2019, and the WEF Annual Meeting 2020 in Davos, focuses on the intersection of technology and consciousness. It is a commentary on the Darwinian nature of mindfulness in a world where technology is designed to manipulate our subconscious mind and daily behaviors. The use of gradient color grounds the observer in their somatic experience, reconnecting the mind and body. She coded and designed #loveincovidtimes as an interactive piece to address mental health during the pandemic. 

Samantha stays awake at night thinking about how data is one of the most harmful or helpful resources on the planet depending on how we wield it. As the Chief Strategy Officer at QEDIT, she focuses on the use of advance cryptographic methods to reduce data burdens, verify information with certainty, and ensure that our data keeps us safe, sovereign, and enlightened. 

Samantha backs startups focused on mental health -- she defines this broadly to include solutions to climate change, or our violent abstraction from our nature. 

Samantha spent years predicting the geographies, verticals, and consequences of emerging technologies globally. At TechCrunch, she helped map and expand their audience by identifying the startup ecosystem influences and seed-stage startups most likely to produce an exit. 

She was named a World Economic Forum Media Leader in 2018, and is also a contributor to Forbes.
anything_else_to_share:
Please reach out if you need support with making your venture’s work easy to digest for a multi-tiered audience (investors, customers, etc).

If you’re interested in learning more about mental health innovation through immersive art and technology, say hello :)

People are good, bad things can happen to any of us.. so why then does the financial system judge people for what life inherently is... unpredictable! Crazy right?

My personal mission is to re-write the playbook of lending and debt. 

I'm the co-founder of the social lender, Money Sweetspot. It is a place where people have a financial reset, repay their debt and get on with their lives. We see a person's value as much more than the cash in their pocket.

We exist for every day, imperfect humans who make mistakes, try their best, and want to be in the driving seat of their own financial lives. 

As background, my executive-level career spans financial services, local government and accounting sectors. I act as a translator between strategy, people and numbers.

My commitment, energy and drive is all about the people; creating communities and cultures of collaboration, courage and reciprocity.

NZ’s leadership was admired globally for its science-based handling of the pandemic.  I believe NZ has the unique opportunity to showcase the same kind of leadership for tackling climate change while leveraging the indigenous wisdom of the Māori. career_highlights: Shama is the former COO of Thinx Inc., the revolutionary period and incontinence underwear company. In her capacity as COO, Shama was responsible for driving multiple functions including strategy, omni-channel sales, demand planning, product innovation, supply chain and project management. She held a key role in Thinx’s transition from a startup to a growth stage company, overseeing operations at a time when Thinx sales grew exponentially. She served as the company’s interim CEO three times and now serves as an advisor.
how_im_looking_to_support_the_nz_ecosyst:
I am keen to expand my work through advising and collaborating with like-minded entrepreneurs, particularly those focused on climate change, women’s health and human rights. I would love to partner with cross-institutional agencies (government, academia, non-profit, private enterprise) to advance entrepreneurship and social innovation.
In addition, I would be interested in joining NZ-based venture networks, and can offer a fairly robust network with US-based venture funds and startups.
key_networks:
Next Gen Venture Partners
Chief
impact_goals_nz:
My driving vision is to change consumer behaviour for the betterment of the planet and society. With Thinx, this meant:
1) Catalysing the mass adoption of a reusable alternative in a category dominated by single-use disposables. 
2) Breaking stigmas around menstruation and incontinence to uplift and empower women globally.
how_to_support_my_work: Any connections to those working towards similar goals would be much appreciated.
five_things_to_know:
Shama was born into a civil war in Sri Lanka, and left just before the 2004 tsunami, made all the worse by climate-induced rises in sea levels. As such, peace and sustainability are very high in her pantheon of values. 

When she joined Thinx as employee #4 in 2015, Shama had just taken a climate change expedition to Antarctica the prior year. There she learned that fashion, the industry that she’d worked in for her entire career, is the world’s second largest polluter after oil; and that the feminine hygiene industry is responsible for 20 billion non-biodegradable pads and tampons that end up in North American landfills each year. Thinx, at the intersection of fashion and feminine hygiene, is a company with a patented product that seeks to change that.
 
In her capacity as COO & CPO, Shama was responsible for driving strategy, sales, product innovation, supply chain, customer experience and partnerships. She held a key role in Thinx’s transition from a startup to a growth stage company, overseeing operations at a time when Thinx sales grew exponentially. She served as the company’s interim CEO three times and now serves as an advisor.
 
Prior to joining Thinx, Shama was based out of Dubai, where she was a merchant for Bath and Body Works International, supporting 10 countries in the Middle East. Previously, she worked in product development and merchandising for L Brands (formerly Limited Brands).

She lives with her husband, Ben, and 2 children, in Brooklyn, NY. Shama is an avid swimmer and a TRX instructor.

career_highlights:
I have extensive experience in development, project management, fundraising, communications and community engagement. I have nearly two decades of expertise in arts and cultural management, specializing in projects that cultivate social change, creative expression, and cultural diversity. I'm driven by my commitment to creating a more equitable and inclusive world.

As the founder and director of The Little Art, I have used art as a transformative tool to educate, inspire, and empower children and youth. Beyond that, my portfolio includes the curation and organization of national and international festivals in film, digital art, design and performing arts that showcase world-class art but also spotlight emerging talents in the arts. 

In the consulting sphere, I've advised organizations like the British Council, UNESCO, and the Government of Sharjah in the UAE. I have helped them shape and execute their arts and cultural strategies, thereby influencing policy on a global scale. Through memberships in prestigious networks and fellowships—including the DeVos Institute of Arts Management, Asia Society, Acumen, and the International Society of Performing Arts and Edmund Hillary Fellowship (EHF) New Zealand — I've collaborated with some of the most innovative minds in the fields of arts, entrepreneurship and social change. 

My skill set spans non-profit management, communication, fundraising, marketing and communication, event management, and arts education, anchored by a solid foundation in strategic planning. But it's not just what I do that's important; it's why I do it. I am fervent about using arts and culture not just as forms of expression but as catalysts for social change and mediums for cross-cultural dialogues.

I live and work in Auckland, New Zealand.
key_networks:

- 2015 Acumen fellow
- 2015 ArtThink South Asia fellow
- 2020 Global Fellow with International Society of Performing Arts

Memberships in Professional Networks 
- ISPA | International Society of Performing Arts
- CIFEJ |  International Centre of Films for Children and Youth
- ASSITEJ | International Association of Theatre for Children and Young People
- SCCF | Southaisan Children's Cinema Forum 
- UNESCO YES Peace | Youth for Education, Sustainability and Peace
- UNESCO GCED Network (Global Citizenship Education (GCED)
impact_goals_nz:
The Little Art, the organisation I founded in Pakistan engaged nearly one million children and youth over the past 16 years. We engaged nearly 150,000 children in creating art and hosted over 40 art exhibitions across the country.

Using this experience, I am launching reEarth Studio in and from New Zealand. 

ReEarth Studio aims to engage children and youth in a global dialogue on climate change through arts, technology, and culture. See the project's core idea here: https://reearth.studio/

In March 2024, the project will be launched to nearly 25,000 schools globally and will have art exhibitions (photography, painting, digital arts) curated by a team of international artists and curators for major venues. 

The mission of reEarth Studio is to foster a worldwide dialogue with children and youth through the power of creation to inspire proactive global stewardship and meaningful ecological actions.
how_to_support_my_work:
I am seeking help in the following areas. 

- Collaborate on Programs: Organizations in the arts, education, and climate change sectors to form partnerships with The Little Art to co-develop and co-host educational and artistic programs, leveraging each other's expertise and reach.

- Expert Advice: Professionals with specialized knowledge in fields like arts education, climate change, and non-profit management serve as advisors to provide strategic direction and specialized insights to The Little Art.

- Financial Support: Grant agencies, philanthropic organizations, and individual donors contribute financially to The Little Art, which is a registered charity in New Zealand. These funds will be allocated to program development, resource procurement, and outreach activities.

- Donate Resources In-kind donations: such as educational materials, art supplies, or event spaces, are welcome and would be utilized to further the project's reach and impact among children and youth.

currently_based_in: Dehradun, UT 248003 India

what_is_your_main_mode_of_working: Advising & mentoring, Connecting, Storytelling, Teaming up with local organizations to strengthen their/our impact
career_highlights:
Avid mountaineer, explorer, youth development activist and peace builder. My vision is to contribute positively to sustainable peace and development, keeping our planet a place for all beings to fully realise their potential. I believe in helping young people especially girls transition into responsible ‘global citizens’ by increasing their awareness and leadership qualities through adventure. And to find peace with in their heart, because ‘peace within is peace without’. 

In 2019, I along with my twin sister (Nungshi) we successfully completed the “Worlds Toughest Race - an ultimate expedition race in which teams race non-stop, 24 hours a day, covering a distance of 671 km in 11 days across hundreds of miles of rugged backcountry terrain complete with mountains, jungles and oceans“ in Fiji. We were the only South Asians amongst 66 experienced and hardened competing teams from 30 countries. This race is featured on Amazon Prime and with Bear Grylls as the Host, is a “must watch” for adventure sport lovers. 

Despite roots in India’s conservative rural Haryana state, notorious for its skewed sex ratio in favour of boys, I and my twin sister Nungshi Malik have achieved several global gender iconic milestones. In 2013, barely 21 years old, we became World’s first female twins to scale Mt. Everest. What’s more, with in just 2 years from that feat we scaled highest peaks in all continents and skied to North and South Poles becoming World’s first siblings and twins, as well as first and only South Asians to complete the ‘Adventurers Grand Slam’. At that time we were also world’s youngest persons to achieve it. We have as many Asian and National records to credit. We are also World’s first female twins to climb Mt Cook, New Zealand’s tallest peak and also scaled a virgin peak at 21000 ft in the Indian Himalayas.
