career_highlights:
KEY CAREER MILESTONES

1. I am the Co-Founder, CEO, Lead Instructor and Lead Consultant of These Hands GSSE which is a for-profit social enterprise based in Gaborone, Botswana and Tolaga Bay, New Zealand. 

2. I am the Co-Founder and Coordinator of the Slash for Cash Project and Tolaga Bay Inn Community Workshop based in Tolaga Bay, New Zealand. 

3. Current Board Chairperson of the South Sudanese Refugee Led Youth Social Advocacy Team (YSAT) Rhino Camp Refugee Settlement in Uganda and in Juba, South Sudan.

4. I am a Founding member of the International Development Design Summit (IDDS)/International Development Innovation Network (IDIN) steering committee based at Massachussets Institute of Technology (MIT) D-Lab. 

5. I was a 2023 Advisory Board Member of the Falling Walls Science Engagement Category Jury; I was 2022 Winner of the Falling Walls Science Engagement Category; and I was a 2019 Falling Walls Top 50 finalist in the Science and Innovation Management Category. 

6. I am a former Chairperson of the Botswana Human Resource Development Council's Creative Industries Sector Committee and a former Vice Chairperson of the Botswana National Commission for UNESCO’s Communication and Information Programme Committee.

7. I am the Co-founder of the IDIN Southern Africa Consortium, Lead instructor/facilitator of the Injini ED Tech Southern Africa Consortium in Botswana, Founding member of The Southern Africa Innovation Colletive Management Team and a Creative Consultant for DSIL Global. 

8. An advisory board member of the Botswana International University of Science and Technology (BIUST)’s Centre for Business Management, Entrepreneurship and General Education (CBMEGE)

9. 2015 Fellow and Lead Partinizer for the Africa Region for the Ashoka Social Intrapreneurship for Innovation in Health and Wellness course

10. 2015 Top 11 semi-finalist of the Orange Africa Social Venture Prize.

11. 2016 winner of the Mail & Guardian Newspaper and Gabz FM Radio’s 50 Batswana under 40 Innovation Award.

12. 2018 World Bank Social Inclusion Heroes Competition Top 27 Finalist. 

13. 2019 ambassador of the Michigan State University’s Global Youth Advancement Network in Botswana. 


THESE HANDS GSSE and IDIN SOUTHERN AFRICA CONSORTIUM

These Hands GSSE is a global for profit social enterprise start-up based in Botswana, which trains and supports rural community innovators/entrepreneurs in developing countries. As an enabler for international development work, we have also developed a social media platform that is dedicated to international development and co-creative design work and does not require internet to use (USSD Based). We recently graduated from incubation at the Botswana Innovation Hub’s First Steps Venture Centre (BIH FSVC) and are now members. 

These Hands GSSE is also a lifelong implementation partner and rural innovation center partner of the International Development Innovation Network (IDIN) and the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) D-Lab for Botswana, Africa, Latin America, South East Asia and other parts of the World. 

These Hands GSSE also an implementation partner of the Botswana International University of Science and Technology (BIUST) through its first university chapter being the Via Maranyane Student Organization and of the Injini Education Technology Incubation Programme for Botswana. 

In 2017, These Hands co-founded the International Development Innovation Network (IDIN)-SADC Consortium with partners Kafue Innovation Center in Zambia and Twende Social Innovation Center in Tanzania.

IDIN Southern Africa (International Development Innovation Network – Southern Africa) is a regional consortium led by an Executive Committee (EC) of these grassroots innovation ecosystem builders around SADC region of Africa.  

The EC veers away from traditional top-down approaches to development and technology intervention and instead leads a bottom-up movement of grassroots communities who demonstrate interest in innovation.

The EC partners with these communities to co-create appropriate technologies that address immediate livelihood challenges, to set up small ventures around these technologies, and to develop innovation centres to continue community-driven innovation work. 

These Hands serve as the secretariat of the IDIN Southern Africa consortium pilot project and as thus administers the overall consortium grants and other resources for implementation of work in the 5 SADC countries of Botswana, Zambia, Tanzania, Namibia and South Africa. 

All in all These Hands and IDIN Southern Africa has trained over 2000 grassroots innovators spread across 14 rural innovation centers and 30 chapters in the aforesmentioned Southern African countries. 

We are on a mission to train, empower and support at least 1 billion people by the year 2061. 

SLASH FOR CASH 

The Slash for Cash Project which is an initiative of the Tolaga Bay Inn Charitable Trust (TBICT) is on a mission to clean and heal the Tairawhiti and Wairoa regional lands by repurposing wood debris waste (slash) on the forestry lands, farmlands, orchards, beaches, roadsides and landfills into organic biochar fertilizer and smokeless charcoal briquettes; whilst also creating employment opportunities for the local community.
how_im_looking_to_support_the_nz_ecosyst: I am eager to support the Kiwi Innovation Ecosystem by building the New Zealand Grassroots Innovation Ecosystem and linking it to the National Innovation Ecosystem whilst also sharing my expertise, knowledge, skills, resources, networks and opportunities in my other spaces where I often times do work.

impact_goals_nz:
Through These Hands New Zealand we intend to partner with the Māori Community and Kiwi Innovation Ecosystem to co-create appropriate technologies that address immediate livelihood challenges, to set up small ventures around these technologies, and to develop innovation centres to continue community-driven innovation work in New Zealand, Oceania, South East Asia, Indo-Asia, the Mediterranean and Eastern Europe. 

Key elements of my proposition are:

● Build It Workshops: Ranging from a couple of hours to 7 days, Build It trainings teach participants how to make specific low-cost, useful technologies through step-by-step directions. Afterwards, participants use these technologies in their homes and share them with their communities. Some examples include a hand maize Sheller, a charcoal press, and a PET plastic bottle cutter. Curriculum for Build It training is sourced from around the world and adapted to local materials and conditions.

● Creative Capacity Building Training Workshops: 5-10 day co-creation training workshops amongst people who stay together in the same village, school, or organization and face the same livelihood challenges. Participants are taught the co-creative design and business thinking processes and supported with materials and tools to co-create prototypes and ventures that address their own livelihood challenges. 

● Innovation Centres: rural community based co-working spaces that allow our summit participants or CCB participants to be able to continue working on their prototypes and ventures by accessing all the material, tools and technical support necessary. 

●  International Development Design Summits: 2-5 week hands-on design summits that bring a diverse group of participants from different countries and background to co-create appropriate technologies and ventures with local communities in New Zealand and Oceania region. 		

● Project Support Grants: competitive small grants to help in assisting our innovators to be able to buy material and tools to design or iterate their technology projects or paying for the essential steps in their business development. 

*Small grants as small as 150 USD 	

● Chapter Meetings: small meetings by our innovators to either train each other a new skill or give feedback on a new design. 	

● Cross Border Exchange Visits/Exposure Trips: members from Oceania members states will get an opportunity to travel to each other’s working spaces to either learn or impart new skills about a project; and to exhibit and trade their technologies or products of their technologies on calendar dates that members will agree upon that are strategic at the time.

● Exhibitions: innovators and entrepreneurs at innovation centres showcase their technologies to the general public for sales, feedback, and inspiration.

● USSD Based Social Network Platform: innovators and entrepreneurs at innovation centres, in countries we have a foot print in and from across our global network are able to access our pre and post training services and able to connect to each other by dialling *247# on their GSM and Smartphones using only USD0.20 of their airtime per session without requiring internet.
how_to_support_my_work:
All our planned activities will require patient capital investors to help us scale our work to reach at least 1 billion of the 4 billion people living the poverty datum line of 10 USD per day in rural communities; indigenous people communities and refugee camps by the year 2061. 

We will also require impact investors who will help us subsidize our trainings and activities for our innovators. 

But more importantly, we will require collaborators and eager communities who are ready to engage with us and our approach to make a difference in their own lives. 

Lastly, we will require stakeholders, angel investors and venture capitalists that can support our innovators to commercialise their solutions for greater impact and returns.

five_things_to_know:
I am a creative synergist traversing multiple domains. Driven by curiosity and the desire for positive world change, my journey has taken the form of a high stakes adventure, a quest for solution-oriented narratives that will guide our civilization into a regenerative future. Through the arts we can inspire a renaissance – a new enlightenment towards an ecological civilization – one that envisions a new earth wherein planetary citizens regenerate earths living systems and pursue peace with all life. To captivate the public imagination, a mythical, whole-system vision for storytelling that connects head to heart is key.  
 
I am Executive Director at Compathos, an arts and media nonprofit, home of IMRSIV>Storylab, and Films for the Planet- the best in environmental and social action films on demand. Films for the Planet supports the work of filmmakers, acting in a promotional capacity connecting people to films that will assist them in their journey as catalysts for positive change. I facilitate a global collective of world-class artists, filmmakers and entrepreneurs while creating online publishing platforms with media-rich learning environments and on-demand content. Our portfolio of projects includes a series of branded film events in the San Francisco Bay Area as well as media projects originating in Ethiopia, Brazil, Costa Rica, China, Indonesia, and Standing Rock.

Author, speaker, media strategist and story weaver – I specialize in crafting immersive experiences that harness the shifting nuances of cultural engagement. I’ve produced award-winning films and books, long-form interactive, media expeditions, art exhibitions, expositions, film festivals, virtual learning journeys and streaming film events with significant reach. Projects also include impact reports using data visualization to document how large-scale film campaigns with multi-stakeholder engagement affect whole systems transformation. 

As a Network Weaver Consultant, I evaluate tools and platforms for mapping innovation ecosystems, network communications, convenings and distribution, social systems mapping and analysis, and sense-making through story pattern identification. Weaving between a constellation of global transformation networks, I thrive in diverse network leadership and creative capacities. Networked movements that accelerate collective impact will be among the top future trends. System architecture and permaculture - creating the conditions for a thriving network culture will ultimately define a networks success.  

Through various roles as a creative director and marketing director, I’ve cultivated skill sets in strategic communications design, campaign design, and user experience. My digital art repository, The Twelfth Muse, is a visual poetry collection exploring the use of complex data graphics, nature’s fractals, lyrical myth and transcendent archetypes.  I’ve also written, illustrated and composed the music for a children’s sci-fi fantasy book which I aim to recast as an animated film. 
   
Little known facts: I was the architectural, interior, functional, lighting and landscape designer for our home in the California Sierra foothills. My husband and I own Millennia Music & Media Systems – with over 35 industry awards, a world leader in critical audio recording products and the home of IMERSIV - a new audio technology paradigm.

five_things_to_know:
I am an engineer from Taranaki, New Zealand. I returned to New Zealand in late 2019 to start EnergyBank, a venture that will catalyze deep penetration of renewable energy globally. I have spent the previous four years running my own engineering consultancy in the USA and Mexico. My professional experience to date has been in the electricity and manufacturing sectors. This is my third venture and to date has been funded by my previous company.

EnergyBank was established to eliminate the technical and economic limitations caused by intermittent supply of energy from the most scalable renewables, wind and solar. We will do this by developing and offering energy storage hardware as a service. The hardware we are developing is scalable to the size of the problem, each mass-produced unit will store the equivalent amount of energy as today's largest pumped hydro facilities. It will do this while requiring very little space, having negligible negative environmental effects, and performing with very high round-trip efficiency. We are offering this hardware as a service as it will allow for faster, lower-risk uptake and utilization by risk adverse incumbents in the electricity sector.

five_things_to_know:
Tim has 20 years experience inventing, creating and delivering quality innovative products following graduation with an MA in industrial product design and reflective design practice from Northumbria University, UK. Starting his career at the Ministry of Defence, in concept generation and machine prototyping gas respirators for UK & USA military all of Tim’s inventions are either in production or clinical trial stage and span across fields from consumer goods to medical devices and has received 11 national and international awards for his designs, which include gold for ‘Best Innovation 2009/2010’ – for the Baby Bottle and Gold for best design and innovation, Langtons No.1 Gin 2013 and more recently the 2019 Yahoo Sports Technology award for 'Best technology for athlete welfare'. Recent companies Tim co-founded include https://www.hp1t.com/ which develops IoT sensor systems to collect and determine impact and pressure data.  Current projects:  
(1)Vorte Technologies. Vorte's vision is to mitigate human impact on climate change helping both the environment and future generations by introducing a platform technology that is run from just compressed air to replace traditional cooling, freezing and heating applications. No traditional refrigeration cycle involved, meaning no environmental impact with zero emissions beyond air and is carbon negative. Initial application is focused for integration into the cold chain supply in rural areas and the Gas Distribution Network. (2) A discovery and feasibility study resulting in a business plan for his client relating to the introduction of advanced recycling plants in NZ diverting post consumer waste plastics from landfill and converting the feedstock into valuable pyrolysis oil contributing to the polymer circular economy.

career_highlights:
Tina Jennen is hardwired to support the success of the collective, connecting and amplifying the work of others.  She utilises her career as a learning journey and has primarily functioned as an impact intrapreneur throughout her portfolio approach.  

After moving in 2010 to the Bay of Plenty, Aotearoa New Zealand with her four children, she launched leased executive consultancy, Zealand Zest, to support capability development.  Tina has since held several local executive and governance roles, while also supporting merger-acquisitions and joint ventures (primarily in horticulture technology).  

She also led "Plenty of Innovation" at Enterprise Angels as part of her MBA action research, publishing a dissertation titled "Engaging the Entrepreneurial Ecosystem" to better understand how to create more local deal flow for angel investors.  

Tina believes we have a duty to actively support capability development for the next generation.  Over the past 10 years, she has supported applied learning as a guest lecturer-practitioner.  Most recently, Tina is serving a mix of for-profit and not-for-profit board roles to better understand the governance continuum in conjunction with her PhD pathway.  

Tina's transdisciplinary career approach allows her to more easily identify opportunities to support local initiatives that foster engagement and collaboration to create new community connection and infrastructure. 

Previous US-based initiatives include:  
1. supporting a 70-acre sustainable planned unit development (PUD) based on New Urbanism design principles, preserving 75% of the mature trees, restoring 10-acres of wetland cells, and governing other common-pool resources to demonstrate the symbiosis of neighbourhood and nature.
2. fundraising and development for the very first company in the US to implement direct person-to-person communication on a large scale to effect political change across a broad array of issues for up to 30 different 501(c)(3)s.
3. numerous finance roles including: financial planner/stock broker (Series 7) for large Midwest group practice and sole accountant for AWSNA.
how_im_looking_to_support_the_nz_ecosyst: Leveraging 30 years of diverse and practical business experience to amplify ventures that foster wellbeing and support systems change.
impact_goals_nz: Tina is involved behind-the-scenes with supporting both social sector and entrepreneurial ecosystem development.
five_things_to_know: I grew up in South Dakota; my father came from the Sioux Tribe.  I am a proud mother of four and chose to raise my family in New Zealand.  I'm hardwired to support the success of others, so I've functioned more like an impact intrepreneur.  I've used my portfolio career as an action-learning playground and am now deepening my learning and service through a PhD pathway.  I'm insanely optimistic and my brain doesn't easily shut off.

career_highlights:
Tony tunes into collective potential at a human and a systems level. He is a lawyer, researcher, and technology entrepreneur, advising and working with companies, government agencies, law firms, and nonprofits, bridging from new approaches and tools at the intersection of law and technology to practical use cases in community sovereignty, data governance, compliance, and the management of common risks and resources at a global and local scale.

He is an Entrepreneurial Fellow and Founder of the Blockchain Group at CodeX, Stanford's Center for Legal Informatics. Tony co-founded the company, Legal.io, to deploy technology to scale legal access worldwide, and serves on the boards of various companies and non-profit organizations working on improving data governance and legal inter-operability. He consults on collaboration design with DSIL Global and learnt design thinking at Stanford’s d.school. He helped design and research the first legal technology course at Stanford Law School, and was on the founding team of StartX, the Stanford-affiliated startup accelerator. He advises government agencies, startups, enterprises, and legal service organizations at the intersection of computational law, smart legal contracts, digital identity, and service technologies and protocols.

Prior to his work at Stanford, Tony clocked 10,000 hours’ experience as a lawyer advising companies, public sector bodies and charities on regulatory, transactional and IP issues in Europe, Asia and Africa. He holds bachelor’s and master’s degrees from Oxford University, and a Master of Laws degree in Law, Science and Technology from Stanford.
how_im_looking_to_support_the_nz_ecosyst: Mentorship around startups and startup ecosystems. Connecting people and projects working at the intersection of climate, biodiversity, blockchain, and local sovereignty/governance.
key_networks:
StartX - Stanford startup community.
Legal.io - Global legal talent network.
CodeX / law.mit.edu / LegalHackers - Computational law and policy communities.
Foresight Institute - Transformative technology community.
Embassy Network - Global network of coliving communities.
Wilbur Nature Workshops - Nature, biodiversity, technology, and politics focused workshops and gatherings.
Metagov - Future of governance, DAOs, and Web3 focused community of communities.
impact_goals_nz:
We are facing a planetary crisis: To prevent climate disaster and support a healthy biosphere, we need to transform political and economic incentives. “Ecocoins” are theorized financial mechanisms that can create new, scalable (or replicable) incentive structures. Developers in an emerging ecocoin ecosystem are innovating in the broader field of climate finance and governance through new applications of cryptocurrency, blockchain, smart contracts, and peer-to-peer technologies (collectively, “Web3”). 

My goal in Aotearoa NZ is to nurture partnerships and prototypes that can help to demonstrate the potential of this emerging ecosystem and help to weave these projects together on a global stage, looking beyond institutions towards bottoms-up relationships, participation, sovereignty, and stewardship.
how_to_support_my_work:
Share your stories and work around climate, biodiversity, blockchain, and local sovereignty/governance. 
Support our work as a philanthropic funder and/or volunteer.
five_things_to_know:
Lead a Stanford-based research lab on Computational Law and Blockchain for Public Goods, with a focus on ecological design patterns and principles, and related technical and legal infrastructure.

Former international corporate and commercial lawyer.

Led a technology startup to Series A (Legal.io), and on the founding team of a top-tier entrepreneurship accelerator (StartX).

Co-create and co-steward co-living communities around the world, including in San Francisco (embassynetwork.com) and France (feytopia.com).

Love to jam with my violin to jazz, techno, folk, or your music of choice!

five_things_to_know:
I am a systems entrepreneur with 10+ years of diverse leadership experiences, passionate about utilising the power of entrepreneurship to collectively solve the global climate emergency and the global refugee crisis. 

I co-founded Catalysr in 2016, a startup pre-accelerator in Australia & New Zealand, which empowers migrant and refugee entrepreneurs (aka migrapreneurs) to launch their own startups. In our first 5 years, Catalysr has supported 400 migrapreneurs launch 150 enterprises in Australia and our aim is to double that impact in the next 2 years by scaling across Australia and New Zealand. 

I am also a Commercialisation Facilitator with the Australian Government's Entrepreneurs' Programme supporting startups to get access to guidance and Accelerating Commercialisation grant, and the Head of Growth and Partnerships in a newspace startup called SpaceBase, with a mission to democratise access to space.

Apart from EHF, I am a Startmate 'First Believer', 2020 Stanford GSB alumnus, a 2019 Obama Leader, WEF Global Shaper, 2019 Westpac Social Change Fellow, 2018 AMP Tomorrow Maker, and a Singularity University's GSP 2017 Fellow.

For my work I was named the 2018 Commonwealth Young Person of the Year amongst 1.4 billion young people living in the 53 Commonwealth nations. I was also recently named a winner in the 'Entrepreneurship' category, for 40 under 40 Most Influential Asian-Australian Leadership Awards 2020.

currently_based_in: Rio Grande,  Puerto Rico

career_highlights:
Vj Anma is a distinguished entrepreneur, investor, and blockchain expert, with a proven track record of success in the tech industry. As the founder and managing director of Genesis Vault Capital, a blockchain investment fund, Vj is at the forefront of the emerging asset class of cryptocurrencies. He has also recently embarked on a new venture in the Web3 space, as the founder of Emergence DAO, a Web3 venture studio that focuses on incubating and accelerating innovative blockchain-based startups.

His expertise in blockchain technology is backed by years of experience and achievements. In 2018, he sold his Cryptoasset Accounting and Tax (CAAT) software to Ernst and Young, cementing his position as a leading innovator in the field. Before that, he co-founded and served as CEO of IdeaMarket, a crowd-sourced marketplace that connected entrepreneurs with pre-funded concepts. The company was backed by prominent angel investors, including Steve Case and Dave McClure. He has also made a name for himself as a startup mentor, guiding early-stage companies on product-market fit, fundraising, and team building.

Vj's entrepreneurial journey began early in life when he sold his first company at the age of 22. He went on to found and sell a series of other successful startups, including the mobile messaging platform AppMe and the visual search engine company Yometa. He also played a key role in the founding of Getaround, a peer-to-peer car-sharing company, where he currently serves on the advisory board and was awarded a patent for his core technology.

He is a highly educated individual, with a master's degree in Computer Science from the University of Illinois at Chicago and a graduate of the inaugural class at Singularity University. He also holds an undergraduate degree in Computer Science and Engineering from the University of Madras, India. Vj's intellectual prowess is further evident in his membership in Mensa International, the high-IQ society.

Beyond his entrepreneurial and intellectual pursuits, Vj is also an advocate for social and environmental causes. He was awarded an honorary doctorate for his efforts in finding homes for children affected by the 2004 Indian Ocean tsunami. He is also a certified yoga instructor, and lifelong meditator, and leads a plant-based lifestyle.

Vj's impressive portfolio of achievements and expertise in the blockchain space make him an invaluable asset to any project or company in need of guidance and innovation.

2. Believe in power of Blockchain to bring financial inclusivity for all.
3. Masters Degree in Computer Science from University of Illinois at Chicago (2000)
4. Graduate of Singularity University (2009).
5. Live in Puerto Rico with a loving wife and hilarious dog.

how_im_looking_to_support_the_nz_ecosyst: I hope to have the opportunity to address the bespoke needs of innovators in New Zealand and members of the Fellowship. I am especially excited to support New Zealand companies in an advisory and mentoring capacity as the global economy shifts from a credit-based volume economy to a fundamentals-based value economy.
impact_goals_nz: By the time the Fellowship starts, I will have finished HowDo’s primary global corpus. Therefore, when I arrive in NZ I look forward to touring the entire country while learning more about the needs of EHF Fellows, NZ entrepreneurs, NZ businesses and NZ communities. Once I have a clear understanding of NZ’s needs - I will look to expand my research to create a bespoke approach towards making a positive impact in NZ’s innovation economy.

five_things_to_know:
Having led innovation at Amazon, PayPal, Rosetta Stone, Target, Visa and several startups, I am building the world’s largest innovation resource: HowDo. HowDo gives people the process, tools (accelerator, communities of practice, culture, incubator, M&A, mentorship, product) and deep insights required to successfully innovate. 360K+ people from 190 countries have learned from HowDo. HowDo's resources are free because knowing how to compete and win in business is a foundational pillar of equal opportunity in every capitalist society; and innovation is the best way to compete and win in business. 

Innovation is also the best way to create quality jobs. Quality jobs are those that provide employees with a living wage, essential benefits, reasonable hours and diverse, inclusive and safe work environments. Hundreds of millions of quality jobs must be created to recover from the pandemic recession, eliminate wealth inequality and decelerate climate change. I want to help so I built HowDo.

five_things_to_know:
Planet Overview
Planet Labs builds and operates fleets of compact, highly capable Earth-imaging satellites – collecting data about the changing planet with a range of humanitarian, commercial and government applications. Founded by NASA scientists, today Planet operates over 150 satellites -- with the capacity to image the entire Earth land mass every day at 3-5m resolution. Planet also operates 21 high resolution (0.5m) satellites capable of stills or video. There are a wide range of humanitarian & science applications such as tracking deforestation, illegal fishing, water security, Earth science; government applications, such as border security, disaster response and urban planning; as well as commercial applications such as improving precision agriculture, consumer mapping, commodities tracking and news. Planet has its principal offices in San Francisco and Berlin. 

Website: www.planet.com

Bio
Prior to Planet, Will was a Scientist at NASA/USRA where he developed a low-cost planetary bus, was Deputy System’s Engineer on lunar orbiter mission “LADEE”, a Science Team member for the lunar impactor mission “LCROSS”, served as Co-Principal Investigator on PhoneSat, and was the technical lead on research projects in space debris remediation. Will has published over 30 articles in scientific publications including Science magazine and he has written a variety of space related op-eds including in the New York Times and Boston Globe.
 
Will received his Ph.D. in Physics from the University of Oxford and his Masters in Physics with Space Science and Technology from the University of Leicester. Will was a Postdoctoral Fellow at George Washington University and Harvard University.
